risky_phi_b_tail_reconsideration.summary: Completed Υ-A closes the earlier release-control doctrine gaps and makes Φ-B3 reviewable, but does not automatically clear Φ-B4 or Φ-B5.

selected_upsilon_b_operationalization.summary: A narrow Υ-B band is now supportable because release, update, trust, and operator substrate already exists in repo code and tools.

zeta_blocker_reduction.summary: Υ-A materially reduces control-plane ambiguity, but Ζ remains blocked by coexistence, hotswap, distributed authority, publication/trust execution maturity, and stronger continuity receipts.

ambiguities_eliminated: completed upsilon-a does not automatically clear the whole risky phi-b tail
more release or control-plane work is not automatically better than reopening runtime review
zeta is not now ready for full planning just because upsilon-a completed

risky_phi_b_tail_readiness.title: MULTI_VERSION_COEXISTENCE-0
risky_phi_b_tail_readiness.summary: Versioning, release contract, release-index, operator transaction, archive, and publication/trust doctrine now exist, but ownership-sensitive roots and compatibility-boundary cautions remain active.

risky_phi_b_tail_readiness.title: HOTSWAP_BOUNDARIES-0
risky_phi_b_tail_readiness.summary: Hotswap still needs coexistence findings plus stronger operational receipts around lifecycle, rollback, parity, and operator authority.

risky_phi_b_tail_readiness.title: DISTRIBUTED_AUTHORITY_FOUNDATIONS-0
risky_phi_b_tail_readiness.summary: Distributed authority still lacks authority handoff, proof-anchor quorum, distributed replay verification, and trust-sensitive operational maturity.

upsilon_b_readiness.title: RELEASE_OPS_ALIGNMENT-0
upsilon_b_readiness.summary: Existing operator, release, and control-plane surfaces already form a real substrate that can now be aligned without reinvention.

upsilon_b_readiness.title: MANUAL_AUTOMATION_PARITY_AND_REHEARSAL-0
upsilon_b_readiness.summary: Parity adapters, tests, and rehearsal expectations already exist and should be formalized before later cutover-sensitive runtime review.

upsilon_b_readiness.title: OPERATOR_TRANSACTION_IMPLEMENTATION_ALIGNMENT-0
upsilon_b_readiness.summary: Install and update transaction logs exist, but broader operator-ledger alignment remains only partially embodied and review-aware.

upsilon_b_readiness.title: RELEASE_PIPELINE_IMPLEMENTATION_ALIGNMENT-0
upsilon_b_readiness.summary: Manifest, index, archive, and dist substrate already exists; this is real but not the immediate next blocker after Υ-A.

upsilon_b_readiness.title: ARCHIVE_MIRROR_OPERATIONAL_ALIGNMENT-0
upsilon_b_readiness.summary: Archive and mirror doctrine is frozen and the repo already has archive policy substrate.

upsilon_b_readiness.title: DOWNGRADE_AND_YANK_EXECUTION_ALIGNMENT-0
upsilon_b_readiness.summary: Downgrade, rollback, and yanked-selection behavior already exists and has been constitutionalized in Υ-A.

upsilon_b_readiness.title: LICENSE_CAPABILITY_AND_TRUST_DISTRIBUTION_ALIGNMENT-0
upsilon_b_readiness.summary: Trust policies are provisional and trust roots are still empty, so this family remains dangerous to operationalize.

upsilon_b_readiness.title: PUBLICATION_MODEL_OPERATIONAL_ALIGNMENT-0
upsilon_b_readiness.summary: Publication and exposure remain gated and must not be operationalized casually from feeds, mirrors, or existing release outputs.

zeta_blockers_or_prerequisites.summary: Ζ still needs lawful coexistence semantics before live protocol, schema, or module evolution can be treated as safe.
Replaceability, partial reload, and restartless continuity still need explicit safe boundaries.
Authority handoff, distributed replay verification, and quorum semantics remain unresolved.
Later rollout and rehearsal work still needs stronger operational alignment over existing control-plane tooling.
General operator transaction receipts remain narrower than the later live-cutover and disaster-control needs.
Later cutover-sensitive work still needs deterministic parity and rehearsal discipline across operator surfaces.
Publication remains gated, trust policies are provisional, and trust roots are empty.
Replay, snapshot, and isolation are explicit doctrine, but not yet proven live-cutover or distributed machinery.
Index, archive, and rollback substrate exists, but broader operator and publication receipts are not yet generalized.

must_avoid_replacing: canonical release identity and release contract profile semantics
canonical operator transaction and downgrade doctrine
publication, trust, and licensing gate semantics

ownership_projection_cautions: fields/ remains canonical semantic field substrate while field/ remains transitional
schema/ remains canonical semantic contract law while schemas/ remains a projection or validator-facing mirror
packs/ remains canonical for runtime packaging and activation scope while data/packs/ remains scoped authored-pack authority
canonical prose and semantic specs outrank projected or generated mirrors
the thin runtime/ root is not canonical by name alone
release/control-plane convenience must not infer canon or permission
older planning numbering drift remains evidence rather than authority

final_verdict.reason: Υ-A is sufficient to reopen Φ-B3, but not sufficient to clear Φ-B4 or Φ-B5. A narrow Υ-B band should follow Φ-B3 before any move deeper into the risky runtime tail.

notes_on_planning_drift: Older planning mirrors still use pre-refresh Υ numbering and Φ-12..Φ-14 titles.
This checkpoint treats stable family identifiers and the active prompt chain as authoritative over that stale numbering.
